meaning: adv.轻飘地,幽雅地,空气一般地
examples: Halle Berry floats ethereally above a pale pink cloud of fluffiness.
Wood windows and doors makes you building classicality and ethereally with European wood windows and doors.
Her version of Handel's "Let the Bright Seraphim" was ethereally light, as was Sullivan's "Three Little Maids from School".
Two years old, it still has everything I need in a computer, including an ethereally comfortable keyboard.
The city's lights project ethereally across the starry, cloudless sky.
